title: The Great God Quay: The Tale of Barada and the Weequays

"'''The Great God Quay: The Tale of Barada and the Weequays'''" by [[George Alec Effinger]] tells the story of [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]] guard [[Barada/Legends|Barada]] and two [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]] skiff guards. It was published in ''[[Tales from Jabba's Palace]]'' [[December]] [[1995]] by [[Bantam Spectra]].

==Plot summary==
[[Barada/Legends|Barada]] uncovers the body of [[Ak-Buz]] stashed in the [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsor]] garage of the [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|palace]]. There are no marks on the [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]] [[Sail barge/Legends|sail barge]] [[Captain/Legends|captain's]] body that would indicate the cause of [[Death/Legends|death]]. The other Weequay, mad and reverent toward a simple [[Quay novelty toy|child's toy]] they regard as an avatar of their [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Deity/Legends|god]], try to piece together the mystery behind the murder. They ask the toy – which the call a [[Quay (deity)|Quay]] – a series of simple yes or no questions, and the device answers at random in the affirmative, the negative, or with noncommittal deflection. Following a string of lucky questions and answers from the Quay, the Weequay discover and defuse a [[Bomb/Legends|bomb]] aboard [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba's]] sail barge, only to get blown up anyway when [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] destroys the vessel while rescuing his friends.

==Continuity==
The [[Quay novelty toy|Quay]] is essentially an analog of the [[Wikipedia: Magic 8 Ball|Magic 8 Ball]] toy manufactured by [[Mattel]], and even uses the same "Reply hazy. Try again" response. [[Barada/Legends|Barada]] is described as a [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinan]], but the spelling of that species name has been revised to "Klatooinian" in more recent materials.<ref name="TERC" />
